title: Parameters, search state and not-found views

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Parameters, search state and not-found views; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Router Documentation; stable declarative mode accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Parameters, search state and not-found views
sources.claim: Route parameters and search values are untrusted URL strings that need decoding, validation, defaults, and canonicalization.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Route parameters and search values are untrusted URL strings that need decoding, validation, defaults, and canonicalization.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A numeric page parameter is parsed, range-checked, and replaced with a safe default when invalid. Your goal is to implement and test parameterized and filterable views with invalid and unknown URLs. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Remote data and asynchronous UI; javascript browser url; Declarative routes and links. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Route parameters and search values are untrusted URL strings that need decoding, validation, defaults, and canonicalization. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Missing, repeated, malformed, or stale values can select unavailable resources and effects can race. This draft therefore rejects: A typed route declaration guarantees every parameter value is valid domain data.

includes: Parse route and query values as untrusted input and render explicit missing-resource states.
Implement and test parameterized and filterable views with invalid and unknown URLs.
excludes: Missing, repeated, malformed, or stale values can select unavailable resources and effects can race.

outcomes: Implement and test parameterized and filterable views with invalid and unknown URLs.
